heading.title: Building digital systems with structure, clarity, and purpose
heading.desc: Saad ICT was founded on a simple but important observation: many businesses struggle with digital tools that are disconnected from how they actually operate. We exist to bridge that gap.

narrative.para1: Saad ICT was built around a simple observation: many businesses need better systems, not just better-looking software. We focus on platforms and digital tools that support actual operations, improve clarity, and create a stronger foundation for growth.
narrative.para2: Our work spans internal systems, operational platforms, business websites, and service-driven digital infrastructure. Every solution is designed to be practical, scalable, and aligned with how the organization functions in the real world.

platforms.label: Platforms
platforms.value: Operational systems, portals, and internal tools

step01.label: The Operational Gap
step01.description: Many organizations are still relying on disconnected tools, repetitive manual processes, and digital systems that do not reflect how their operations actually work. This creates delays, weakens visibility, and limits their ability to grow with confidence.
step01.technical: Observed: fragmented workflows and low process clarity

step02.label: The Need for Structure
step02.description: Businesses do not just need attractive interfaces. They need digital systems with clear logic, reliable architecture, and workflows that are built around real operational requirements. That means software should support the business properly, not force the business to adapt to weak tools.
step02.technical: Requirement: structured systems with practical business logic

step03.label: The Saad ICT Approach
step03.description: Saad ICT builds platforms, internal systems, and digital tools with a focus on clarity, scalability, and long-term reliability. Our work is rooted in strong architecture, thoughtful user experience, and technical execution that helps businesses operate more professionally.
step03.technical: Execution: scalable systems engineered for real-world use

heading.title: Driven by a clearer long-term direction.
heading.desc: Saad ICT is not only building projects. It is building a stronger digital standard for how businesses in Somalia and beyond can operate.

vision.label: Vision
vision.title: To become a leading ICT company in Somalia and a recognized provider of modern digital systems across the region.
vision.content: The long-term ambition is to help elevate digital expectations in the market — through systems that look modern, work reliably, and support serious business operations.

mission.label: Mission
mission.title: To address the lack of modern, reliable, and user-focused digital systems by delivering software that is structured, scalable, and aligned with real business needs.
mission.content: Saad ICT exists to help businesses move beyond weak digital execution, outdated presentation, and fragmented tools — toward software and systems that support growth, trust, and better day-to-day operations.

heading.title: Systemic Engineering.

data.title: Data Clarity

heading.title: The standards behind how Saad ICT works.
heading.desc: These principles guide how we design, build, and deliver systems that are clear, reliable, and aligned with real business use.

hero.content: At Saad ICT, good delivery is not accidental. It comes from clear principles around structure, usability, reliability, and long-term thinking. These standards shape how we approach every platform, internal tool, and digital system we build.

structure.title: Clear Structure First
structure.text: We start with sound architecture and clear system flow before adding complexity. Strong structure makes everything else more reliable.

quality.title: Quality Over Shortcuts
quality.text: We would rather build carefully than deliver something that looks finished but fails under real use.

reliability.title: Reliability Builds Trust
reliability.text: Systems should feel stable, predictable, and professionally built from day one. Trust comes from consistency in how a product performs.

usability.title: Usability With Purpose
usability.text: Good design is not decoration. It should reduce confusion, support the workflow, and make the product easier to use with confidence.

performance.title: Performance That Matters
performance.text: We focus on performance where it improves the real experience of the system, not just where it looks good in technical summaries.

growth.title: Built To Grow
growth.text: A system should remain useful as the business grows. We think beyond launch and build with future expansion in mind.

data.title: Organized Data Foundations
data.text: Clear data structure improves reporting, reduces duplication, and makes the platform easier to manage over time.

context.title: Business Context Matters
context.text: We do not build in isolation. Every decision should reflect how the team works, how the service is delivered, and what the business actually needs.

heading.title: Built for local relevance, designed with broader reach.
heading.desc: Saad ICT starts with strong understanding of the Somali market while remaining capable of serving clients across different regions and business types.

geoPanel.title: Somalia First
geoPanel.subtitle: Local understanding.
geoPanel.description: Saad ICT is rooted in the realities of the Somali business environment, while maintaining the flexibility to deliver for organizations outside the country as well.
geoPanel.footer: Regional growth remains part of the long-term direction

rail.title: A company built to raise the digital standard.

editorial.lead: Saad ICT was founded with the belief that businesses in Somalia deserve better digital systems — not only better visuals, but better structure, better usability, and better long-term quality.
editorial.paragraph1: The goal is not simply to build websites or applications. The goal is to contribute to a stronger digital environment where businesses can operate with more clarity, trust, and modern capability.
editorial.paragraph2: Saad ICT is still growing, but the direction is clear: raise expectations, build better systems, and deliver work that reflects a more serious standard for the future.

heading.title: The quality benchmark behind the work.
heading.desc: Our Standards represent the core principles that guide every project we undertake. They are the foundation of our commitment to delivering exceptional solutions that drive real business impact.

ux.title: Modern UI / UX
ux.desc: Interfaces should look current, feel clear, and support user confidence from the first interaction.

security.title: Security Awareness
security.desc: Even when a system is simple, the thinking behind it should account for control, integrity, and safer structure.

architecture.title: Scalable Architecture
architecture.desc: The foundation should allow future growth without forcing unnecessary rebuilds too early.

workflow.title: Workflow Alignment
workflow.desc: A strong system matches how a business actually works, instead of forcing weak or generic flows.

performance.title: Performance Discipline
performance.desc: Systems should remain responsive, practical, and focused on useful business outcomes.

delivery.title: Professional Delivery
delivery.desc: Presentation, consistency, and implementation quality all influence how seriously a business is perceived.

heading.title: Feedback shaped by
heading.subtitle: real delivery and working systems.
heading.desc: These testimonials reflect client-facing work across websites, operational platforms, and digital systems. The focus is not only on presentation, but on clarity, structure, and the quality of execution.

heading.title: Built for what comes next,
heading.subtitle: not only for what exists today.
heading.desc: Saad ICT is still in its early stage, but its direction is intentionally larger: stronger systems, a broader portfolio, and a more serious digital standard for the businesses it serves.

hero.title: Initialize
hero.description: Connect with our engineering and systems specialists. We bridge technical gaps with structured architecture and sovereign digital logic.

modal.successDesc: Your email has been received. Our team will respond shortly.
modal.errorDesc: The secure connection was interrupted. Please check your network and try again.

footer.description: We design and build scalable digital systems, platforms, and operational tools that help organizations streamline workflows, improve service delivery, and support long-term growth.

hero.title: Protection with clarity. Service with integrity.
hero.desc: One page. Two views. Read the essentials in Plain English—or switch to the Legal view for full detail. Either way, you’ll find the same commitments: respect for your privacy, fair terms, and transparent service.

safety.title: Safety First
safety.legal: Safety is prioritized in vendor selection and itinerary planning.

honesty.title: Honesty Always
honesty.plain: No hidden tricks, no vague fine print.
honesty.legal: Pricing and inclusions are stated upfront; VAT (5%) included unless stated otherwise.

compliance.title: Compliance
compliance.plain: We follow the rules so your trip goes smoothly.
compliance.legal: We act as agent for flights/hotels/visas and comply with provider and consular policies.

respect.title: Respect
respect.plain: Careful, courteous service—start to safe return.
respect.legal: Staff operate under a duty of care and least-privilege data access.

plain.intro: We’re your agent for flights, hotels, and visas. Final approval and availability come from the providers.
bullets: Travelers must be 18+. If younger, a sponsor (guardian) must travel with them and complete all forms.
Passport must be valid at least 6 months from travel date (12+ months recommended).
All prices include 5% VAT unless we explicitly say otherwise.

legal.intro: Hoggaan Travels acts solely as an agent coordinating reservations and visa applications with third-party providers. All bookings are subject to provider rules and capacity; service delivery and approvals remain the provider’s responsibility.
bullets: Age: 18+. Minors require a traveling sponsor/guardian who assumes liability and submits all documentation.
Passport: minimum validity of 6 months post-departure; 12+ months advised.
Pricing: unless itemized otherwise, charges are VAT-inclusive at 5%.

full.title: Full Payment
full.plain: Pay cash or via bank transfer. Booking is confirmed after funds clear.
full.legal: Full settlement via cash or bank transfer; confirmation follows receipt and reconciliation of funds.

install.title: Installments (SomBank & Amal Bank)
install.plain: If eligible, apply with proof of employment, last 6 months’ bank statements, passport (6+ months), photo, and a sponsor (individual or company).
install.legal: Installment eligibility requires: current employment proof, 6-month bank statements, passport validity > 6 months, applicant photo, and a sponsor. If an individual: sponsor’s passport, photo, employment proof, and 6-month bank statements. If a company: valid registration (Shatiga).

plain: Full refund if you cancel before your visa is submitted to the main office or processed.
If your visa was already submitted or issued, we deduct only the visa cost and refund the rest.
legal: For package cancellations prior to visa submission or processing by the main office, Customer is entitled to a full refund.
Where visa has been submitted to the main office or issued, the visa fee becomes non-refundable. Only that amount is deducted; all other recoverable sums are returned.

identity.ex: Full name, nationality, passport number, photo
identity.why: Visa issuance, traveler verification, itinerary display (Hoggaan ID & QR)

contact.share: Service providers if needed for booking confirmation

booking.share: Airlines, hotels (as required to fulfill reservations)

dataMap.note: We do not use your data for marketing or analytics—only to deliver your trip.

contract.title: Contract
contract.plain: We need some data to book your trip and deliver the services you ordered.
contract.legal: Processing necessary to perform a contract or to take steps at your request before entering a contract.

legal.title: Legal Obligation
legal.plain: We obey applicable laws and consular requirements.
legal.legal: Processing to comply with legal duties, immigration/consular requirements, and tax obligations.

legit.title: Legitimate Interests
legit.plain: We keep your journey secure and running smoothly.
legit.legal: Processing for service integrity, fraud prevention, and secure itinerary delivery—balanced against your rights.

consent.title: Consent (specific features)
consent.plain: If a feature needs consent, we’ll ask and you can withdraw anytime.
consent.legal: Where consent is used, it is specific, informed, and revocable without detriment.

idContact.howLong: While trip is active + legally required period

booking.howLong: While service is active + audit window

install.notes: Only shared with SomBank / Amal Bank for financing.

retention.footer: We only keep data for as long as needed for your journey and our legal duties—no extra.

items.title: Least-Privilege Access
items.body: Only trained Hoggaan staff with a genuine need can access your record.

items.title: Defense-in-Depth
items.body: Controls across people, process, and technology to reduce risk.

items.title: Secure Channels
items.body: Data in transit and at rest is protected with industry-standard methods.

cookies.plain: We avoid tracking. If any cookie exists, it’s essential—like sign-in or language preference. No ads, no analytics.
cookies.legal: Only essential cookies (e.g., session, locale) may be used to deliver core functions. No behavioral ads, cross-site tracking, or marketing analytics are performed.

complaints.plain: If something feels off, tell us—we’ll make it right. If you’re not satisfied, you may contact your local data authority.
complaints.legal: Customers may submit complaints to Hoggaan Travels at the contact below. If unresolved, you may escalate to your competent supervisory authority (where applicable).

changes.change: New dual-mode (Plain / Legal) layout, expanded data map, clarified refunds and installments.
Added QR usage clarifications and cookie stance.

faqHero.title: Questions,
faqHero.description: A practical reference covering the specifics of Saad ICT services, system architectures, delivery timelines, and project development cycles.

terminal.label: FAQ Coverage
terminal.title: What this page covers
terminal.description: This FAQ is designed to answer the practical questions people ask before starting a project or requesting a specialized system.

items.text: What Saad ICT builds and offers

general.title: General Questions
general.description: Common questions about how Saad ICT works, what we build, and how projects typically begin.

items.question: What kind of systems does Saad ICT build?
items.answer: Saad ICT focuses on practical digital systems that solve real operational needs. This includes SaaS platforms, business web applications, mobile apps, informative websites, and structured internal tools such as dashboards, financial systems, workflow systems, and admin platforms.

items.question: Do you only build websites?
items.answer: No. Websites are only one part of what we do. Our main strength is building structured systems and applications — especially platforms that support real business operations, user roles, data workflows, and long-term scalability.

items.question: Can you help shape the idea before development starts?
items.answer: Yes. Many projects begin at the planning stage. We can help define the right scope, structure the workflow, recommend the suitable stack, and identify what should be built first so the project starts on a solid technical foundation.

items.question: Do you work with companies only, or also with startups and individuals?
items.answer: We can work with companies, startups, and serious founders. The important factor is not company size, but whether the project has clear purpose, realistic requirements, and commitment to building it properly.

saas-web-apps.title: SaaS & Web Applications
saas-web-apps.description: Questions related to custom software platforms, dashboards, portals, and operational systems.

items.question: What is the difference between a website and a web application?
items.answer: A website is primarily informational and presentation-focused. A web application is interactive and operational — it includes user actions, dashboards, data handling, workflows, permissions, and business logic.

items.question: Can Saad ICT build a custom SaaS platform for our company?
items.answer: Yes. Custom SaaS development is one of our core service areas. We can build systems tailored to your workflow.

items.question: Do your systems support different user roles and permissions?
items.answer: Yes. Role-based access and structured permission logic are important in most serious systems.

items.question: Can you connect the system to APIs or third-party services?
items.answer: Yes. We can integrate with payment gateways, APIs, maps, storage, and communication tools.

items.question: Do you build internal dashboards and admin panels?
items.answer: Yes. Internal dashboards and control panels are a major part of what we build.

mobile-apps.title: Mobile Applications
mobile-apps.description: Questions about mobile app development and integrations.

items.question: Do you develop for both Android and iPhone?
items.answer: Yes. We build apps for both Android and iOS depending on requirements.

items.question: Can the mobile app connect to our backend?
items.answer: Yes. Apps are connected to APIs, authentication, and real-time systems.

items.question: Can you build apps for staff and customers?
items.answer: Yes. Apps can be internal, customer-facing, or both.

websites.title: Informative Websites
websites.description: Questions about company websites and digital presence.

items.question: What kind of websites does Saad ICT build?
items.answer: We build professional informative websites for companies and organizations.

items.question: Can you redesign an existing website?
items.answer: Yes. We modernize and restructure outdated websites.

items.question: Will the website be mobile responsive?
items.answer: Yes. All websites are responsive across devices.

items.question: Can the website include forms and FAQ sections?
items.answer: Yes. These are standard and fully supported.

process.title: Process & Delivery
process.description: Questions about project flow and execution.

items.question: How does a project usually start?
items.answer: Projects start with understanding requirements, then structuring scope and execution.

items.question: Do you help define the project scope?
items.answer: Yes. Scope definition is a critical part of the process.

items.question: Can a project be built in phases?
items.answer: Yes. Phased development is recommended for complex systems.

items.question: Do you provide support after launch?
items.answer: Yes. Ongoing support and improvements are available.

consulting-ai.title: Consulting & AI
consulting-ai.description: Questions about advisory and AI capabilities.

items.question: What does your consulting include?
items.answer: Consulting includes architecture, workflows, and system planning.

items.question: Do you offer AI solutions?
items.answer: Yes. Focused on practical automation and smart workflows.

items.question: Can AI be added later?
items.answer: Yes. AI is best added after core systems are stable.

projectsCTA.description: Let's skip the fluff. If you have a project that needs technical precision and a solid deployment strategy, we are ready to start.
